title: What Makes DDIM Different
concept: DDIM (Denoising Diffusion Implicit Models) reformulates sampling to allow skipping steps, dramatically reducing the number of network calls needed.

exercises.prompt: In regular diffusion models, how many small denoising steps are typically needed to turn noise into a clear picture?

options.text: Hundreds or even a thousand tiny steps

exercises.explain: Classic diffusion models (DDPM) usually need hundreds to a thousand small steps to slowly clean up the noise, which takes a lot of time!

cards.title: Why Wait 1000 Steps?
cards.body: Imagine baking a cake but you have to check the oven every single second for an hour straight. Exhausting, right? Old diffusion models generate images the same slow way — tiny step after tiny step. DDIM asks: what if we could peek ahead and skip most of that checking, and still get a great cake?

cards.title: What is DDIM?
cards.body: DDIM (Denoising Diffusion Implicit Models) is a smarter way to run diffusion models. It changes the math behind sampling so the model doesn't need to pass through every single noisy step. Instead, it can jump straight from a very noisy image toward a clean one in far fewer steps — while still using the same trained network.

cards.title: Taking the Highway, Not Every Side Street
cards.body: Think of denoising like driving from your house to school. DDPM drives through every single side street and stop sign along the way. DDIM finds a highway shortcut that skips most of the little streets but still ends up at the exact same school. Same destination, way fewer stops!

cards.title: From 1000 Steps to 50
cards.body: A model trained with 1000 denoising steps can, thanks to DDIM, generate a similar-quality image using only 20-50 steps at generation time. That's up to 50x faster image generation, without retraining the model at all!

cards.title: Myth: You Need a New Model
cards.body: Myth: DDIM requires training a totally new, different model. Truth: DDIM reuses the exact same trained network as regular diffusion (DDPM). It only changes the sampling formula — the recipe for how we use the model's predictions to jump between noise levels — not the model itself.

cards.title: Two Ways to Reach the Clean Image
cards.body: Both paths start at pure noise and end at a clear image using the same trained model — they just take different routes to get there.

left.text: Random walk through hundreds/thousands of tiny steps, one at a time, no skipping allowed.

right.text: Deterministic shortcut path that jumps across many steps at once, reaching the same kind of result much faster.

exercises.prompt: What is the main advantage DDIM offers over standard diffusion sampling?

options.text: It uses a completely different neural network
It allows skipping steps, needing far fewer network calls
It only works on text, not images
It removes the need for any training data

exercises.explain: DDIM reformulates the sampling process so we can take bigger jumps between noise levels, drastically cutting the number of steps needed.

exercises.prompt: True or False: DDIM requires training a brand-new model from scratch that is different from a regular diffusion model.
exercises.explain: False! DDIM reuses the same trained diffusion model. It only changes how sampling steps are taken, not the model's training.

exercises.sentence: DDIM lets us ___ steps during sampling, which means far fewer network calls are needed to generate an image.
exercises.explain: DDIM's key trick is allowing the sampling process to skip steps and jump ahead, instead of going through every single one.

exercises.prompt: Which of these best describes what changes when you switch from DDPM to DDIM sampling?

options.text: The sampling formula changes so bigger jumps between steps are possible
The image dataset used for training changes
The neural network architecture is replaced entirely

exercises.explain: Only the sampling math changes — how we use the model's noise predictions to move forward — not the training data or the network itself.

exercises.prompt: A student says: 'DDIM must be a totally separate AI system trained differently, since it makes images so much faster than regular diffusion.' What's wrong with this statement?

options.text: Nothing is wrong — DDIM is indeed trained completely differently
DDIM reuses the same trained model; it just changes the sampling process to allow step-skipping
DDIM doesn't actually make generation faster at all
DDIM only works for audio, not images, so the comparison is meaningless

exercises.explain: DDIM's real innovation is in the sampling math — reformulating how steps connect so we can skip many of them — while using the exact same trained diffusion network as before.
